CONTENTS
BANKS AND BANKING
Chapter 1. An Act pertaining to banking and to amend
and re -enact Section 584 -a and Section 595 of
the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition , 1930 ,
to permit banks and trust companies or com-
bined banks and trust companies , organized
under the laws of this commonwealth , to avail
themselves of powers conferred by Acts of
Congress of March 9 , 1933 , as amended June
15 , 1933 (Banking Conservation Act ) , and June
16 , 1933 ( Banking Act of 1933 ) upon national
banks doing business in this commonwealth ;
and declaring an emergency
CORPORATIONS
Page
Chapter 2. An Act to enable corporations , formed for the
purpose of construction , reconstruction , altera-
tion , or repairs under public regulation or con-
trol of low cost housing and slum clearance
projects as provided under section d of Section
202 of the National Recovery Act of the United
States , and all laws , rules and regulations made
pursuant thereto , and being so incorporated
under the laws of the State of Kentucky , and
whose project has been approved by the city
planning and zoning commission of any city of
this commonwealth having such commission ,
and , where no commission exists , by the legis-
lative body of such city or town , and approved
by the administrator of said National Recovery
Act to condemn any land and its appurtenances
necessary for the carrying out of the said proj-
ect so approved , and to fix the procedure for
such condemnation

warehousemen storing such distilled spirits

providing penalties ; repealing Chapter 166 of
the Acts of the 1926 General Assembly

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GEN-
ERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON TUESDAY , AUGUST
FIFTEENTH , 1933 AND ENDED ON TUESDAY , SEPTEM-
BER TWENTY -SIXTH , 1933 .
CHAPTER 1
AN ACT pertaining to banking and to amend and reenact § 584a
and § 595 of Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition 1930 , to per-
mit banks and trust companies or combined banks and trust
companies organized under the laws of this commonwealth , to
avail themselves of powers conferred by the Acts of Congress
of March 9, 1933 , as amended June 15 , 1933 (Bank Conserva-
tion Act ) , and June 16 , 1933 , ( Banking Act of 1933 ) , upon
National banks doing business in this Commonwealth ; and
declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That § 584a , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition 1930 , ( being § 1 of chapter 16 of the Ses-
sion Acts of 1914 , approved March 4, 1914 ) be and
is hereby amended by adding thereto the following :
" Any bank or trust company or combined bank
and trust company organized under the laws of this
Commonwealth , may subscribe to and own stock of
the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation , and
may subscribe to and become a member of the Tem-
porary Federal Deposit Insurance Fund , and may
take any further steps necessary to obtain the bene-
fits of Federal insurance of its deposits ."

2 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
So that said Section as amended will read as fol-
lows :
" Any bank or trust company organized under the
laws of this Commonwealth may subscribe for and
own stock of the Federal Reserve bank within the
federal reserve district where such bank or trust
company is located and may take any steps neces-
sary to become a member of such federal reserve
bank. Any bank or trust company or combined
bank and trust company , organized under the laws
of this Commonwealth , may subscribe to and own
stock of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corpora-
tion , and may subscribe to and become a member of
the Temporary Federal Deposit Insurance Fund ,
and may take any further steps necessary to obtain
the benefits of Federal insurance of its deposits . "
§ 2. That § 595 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition 1930 ( being § 58 , chapter 171 of the Ses-
sion Acts of 1893 , which became a law April 5 ,
1893 ) , be amended by adding thereto the following :
' Any bank or trust company or combined bank
and trust company heretofore or hereafter organ-
ized under the laws of this Commonwealth may
issue non -assessable preferred capital stock , of one
or more classes , to which, the double liability shall
not apply .
"Such preferred capital stock shall not be con-
sidered as any part of the minimum ' capital stock '
required by § 577 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition 1930 (Acts of 1893 , c . 171 , § 40 ) , but shall be
considered a part of the ' capital stock ' of such bank
or trust company or combined bank and trust com-
pany , for all other purposes , and in the case of exist-
ing corporations shall be issued in the manner now
provided for increasing capital stock .
"The holders of such preferred capital stock shall
be entitled to receive such cumulative dividends at

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 3
a rate not exceeding six per cent . per annum and
shall have such voting and conversion rights , and
such control of management , and such preference
over the holders of common stock in the event of
liquidation , as may be provided in the original or
amended articles of incorporation under which said
stock is issued .
" No issue of preferred stock shall be valid until
the par value of all stock so issued shall be paid in . ”
So that said Section as amended will read as fol-
lows :
"The stockholders of each bank organized under
this article shall be individually responsible , equally
and ratably , and not one for the other , for all con-
tracts and liabilities of such bank to the extent of
the amount of their stock at par value in addition to
the amount of such stock ; but persons holding stock
as fiduciaries shall not be personally liable as stock-
holders , but the estate in their hands shall be liable
in the same manner and to the same extent as the
property of other stockholders ; and no transfer of
stock shall operate as a release of any such liability
existing at the time of such transfer , provided the
action to enforce such liability shall be commenced
within two years from the time of the transfer ; and
the directors of each bank shall , in January of each
year, file with the secretary of state a correct list
of the stockholders and officers of such bank .
"Any bank or trust company , or combined bank
and trust company , heretofore or hereafter organ-
ized under the laws of this commonwealth , may
issue non -assessable preferred capital stock , of one
or more classes , to which the double liability shall
not apply .
" Such preferred capital stock shall not be con-
sidered as any part of the minimum ' capital stock '
required by section 577 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-

4 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
roll's edition 1930 ( Acts of 1893 , c 171 Sec . 40) but
shall be considered a part of the ' capital stock ' of
such bank or trust company , or combined bank and
trust company , for all other purposes , and in the
case of existing corporations shall be issued in the
manner now provided for increasing capital stock .
" The holders of such preferred capital stock shall
be entitled to receive such cumulative dividends at
a rate not exceeding six per cent , per annum and
shall have such voting and conversion rights , and
such control of management , and such preference
over the holders of common stock in the event of
liquidation , as may be provided in the original or
amended articles of incorporation under which said
stock is issued .
"No issue of preferred stock shall be valid until
the par value of all stock so issued shall be paid in . "
§ 3. Because the general depression now exist-
ing , and the privileges and benefits made available
by Acts of Congress known as the Banking Act of
1933 (Act of June 16 , 1933 ) , and the Bank Conser-
vation Act ( Act of March 9 , 1933 , as amended June
15 , 1933 ) , make it necessary to permit banks and
trust companies and combined banks and trust com-
panies , organized under the laws of this Common-
wealth , to compete upon an equal footing with Na-
tional banks , an emergency is hereby declared to
exist , and this Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
9/7/33
Approved :
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 5
CHAPTER 2
AN ACT to enable corporations formed for the purpose of con-
struction , reconstruction , alteration or repairs under public
regulation or control of low-cost housing and slum clearance
projects , as provided under sub -section d , of section 202 of
the National Recovery Act of the United States and all laws ,
rules and regulations made pursuant thereto , and being so
incorporated under the laws of the State of Kentucky , and
whose project has been approved by the City Planning and
Zoning Commission of any city of this Commonwealth having
such Commission , and where no commission exists , by the
legislative body of such city or town , and approved by the
Administrator of said National Recovery Act , to condemn any
land and its appurtenances necessary for the carrying out of
said project so approved , and to fix the procedure for such
condemnation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any corporation organized under the laws
of the State of Kentucky for the purpose of con-
struction , reconstruction , alteration or repairs
under public regulation or control of low -cost hous-
ing and slum clearance projects , in any city of this
State , as provided by Subsection d , of Section 202
of the National Recovery Act of the United States ,
and the laws , rules and regulations made pursuant
thereto and which corporation and its application
for a loan by the United States for such purpose
has been approved and has complied with all the
provisions of this Act , that said corporation be and
it is hereby authorized to condemn in fee simple
any and all property necessary for the accomplish-
ment of such project , all , however , under the con-
ditions hereinafter set forth .
§ 2. For the purposes of this Act , the Planning
and Zoning Commission of any city having a plan-
ning and zoning commission , and where a city has
not such commission , the legislative body of such

6 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
city shall have all the powers of approval and super-
vision hereinafter set forth under the name of the
local body of such city .
3. After the Articles of Incorporation of such
Company has been approved and filed as required
by law , then said Company shall prepare a detailed
plan of the project which they desire to carry out
in such City, and shall submit same to the local body
of such city as herein provided, and shall, if re-
quired , correct and recorrect same until such local
body approves of same . After which approval it
shall submit such project so approved to the Ad-
ministrator under the National Recovery Act or to
such other persons as shall be designated by the Uni-
ted States for that purpose , and if not approved by
such Administrator as approved by such local body
of said City , such Administrator and such local body
may agree with the corporation on changes therein ,
until such local body and such Administrator shall
approve same , and such Administrator shall grant a
loan to such corporation for the carrying out of said
project so approved . Then , and in that event , the
corporation may proceed to condemnation as here-
inabove permitted and under the procedure here-
inafter set forth .
4. The corporation shall first attempt to pur-
chase without condemnation all land and its ap-
purtenances required by said project , but if for any
reason the corporation and the owner or any of the
owners of said land and appurtenances cannot agree
upon a sale , then such corporation , by its board of
directors , shall pass a resolution authorizing and
directing the condemnation by its attorney of such
property . Such resolution, however , before suit is
filed , shall be submitted to the body of said city
designated as hereinbefore , which shall approve
such resolution, if in their opinion such condemna-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 7
tion is necessary and within the purposes of such
project . After which approval , such condemnation
proceedings may be begun in any Circuit or County
Court of said County by petition and summons and
carried on as provided in Section 2852 of the Ken-
tucky Statutes for condemnation of Park property
by the Board of Park Commissioners of any city of
the first class , and subject to all provisions of said
Section applicable thereto , and with right of appeal
from the County Court to the Circuit Court and
from the Circuit Court to the Court of Appeals in
all instances where an appeal is desired .
§ 5. WHEREAS , the purpose of the National
Recovery Act is the immediate use of the money
loaned for the purposes mentioned in this Act in
order that people may be put to work at once and
the National emergency which now exists in refer-
ence to unemployment may be overcome as soon as
possible , an emergency is now declared and this Act
shall take effect from and after its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
(Neither approved
or disapproved .)
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
CHAPTER 3
AN ACT providing that owners of motor vehicles may register
such vehicles by paying as many twelfths of the annual fee
as there are unexpired months of the year , and declaring an
emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Whenever and wherever the words " motor
vehicle " are used in this Act , they shall include all

8 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
vehicles required to be registered by Chapter 88b ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 edition .
2. Any person who is the owner of a motor
vehicle and who has failed to register said vehicle
for the year 1933 and for that reason registers such
vehicle for only part of the year , the fee shall be as
many twelfths of the annual fee as there are un-
expired months in the calendar year . The county
court clerk of the county in which application is
made for registration of any motor vehicle within
the purview of this act shall require of the appli-
cant for such registration certificate to execute an
affidavit in duplicate in which he swears or affirms
that he has not used such motor vehicle upon the
highways or streets of any city or county in this
state for the months of 1933 for which he did not
have license .
§ 3. The provisions of this Act shall be and re-
main effective until January 1 , 1934 , at which time
its provisions shall cease to be in force .
$ 4. Whereas the owners of many motor vehicles
are in need of such motor vehicles for the transpor-
tation of laborers to places of employment an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become a law and be effective upon its passage and
approval by the Governor of this Commonwealth .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
9/9/33
Approved :
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 9
CHAPTER 4
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation ; providing a license
or excise tax on the business or occupation of manufacturing
distilled spirits , known as whiskey or brandy , or other species
of such double stamped spirits in this State , and /or the busi-
ness or occupation of a warehouseman storing such distilled
spirits ; providing for the distribution of said fund ; providing
penalties for the violation of its provisions ; providing for a
Permit Department , and repealing Chapter 166 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1926 , and enacting in lieu thereof the
following Act , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 166 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of 1926 be repealed , and inserting in lieu
thereof the following Act :
§ 1. Every corporation , association , company ,
co -partnership or individual engaged in the busi-
ness occupation of manufacturing distilled
spirits , known as whiskey or brandy , or other spe-
cies of such double stamped spirits in this State ,
and/or every owner or proprietor of a bonded ware-
house in this State in which such spirits are stored ,
shall , in addition to the taxes now imposed by law ,
pay to the Commonwealth of Kentucky a license tax
of fifty cents ( 50c ) on every proof gallon of distilled
spirits manufactured prior to January 1 , 1928 , and
two cents ( 2c ) on every proof gallon of said distilled
spirits from January 1 , 1928 , to the date of the
effective repeal of the Eighteenth Amendment ,
which is liable for tax to the Federal Government ,
as shown by its official records , and the amount of
tax due the State shall be thereby fixed and meas-
ured by the State Tax Commission .
2. Every corporation , association , co -partner-
ship or individual engaged in distilling such spirits
as are described above and/or every owner or pro-

10 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
prietor or possessor of bonded warehouse in which
distilled spirits as above described are stored , shall
on the first day of January , May and September ,
each year, make a report to the Auditor of Public
Accounts , in writing , sworn to by the person making
the report , or if the owner or proprietor or pos-
sessor be a corporation , such report shall be made
and sworn to by its principal officer or the manager
in charge . Such report shall show , in proof gal-
lons , the quantity of such spirit on which the gov-
ernment tax has been paid , or has become due, or
transferred under bond or removed from the ware-
house during the preceding four months , and
shall , at the time of making the report , pay to the
Treasurer , through the Auditor , the aforesaid tax
per proof gallon on such distilled spirits reported.
on as above directed . All distilled spirits liable to
taxation under this Act now in bonded warehouses
in this State will be subject to this tax the same
as such spirits which shall be hereafter distilled , or
stored in such warehouses , and the State shall have
a lien for license tax due , which shall follow any
change of ownership .
$ 3. It shall be the duty of each firm, corpora-
tion , association , co -partnership or individual en-
gaged in the business of manufacturing distilled
spirits , or owner or proprietor of a bonded ware-
house , in the State , to make a report on the first day
of October , One Thousand Nine Hundred and
Thirty -three , showing the total number of proof
gallons of such distilled spirits as above described.
in each and every bonded warehouse owned or con-
trolled by such person , firm, corporation , associa-
tion , or individual , and at the same time give the
capacity of the distillery . This report to be in addi-
tion to the report for license tax paying purposes as
above provided .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 11
§ 4. On the failure of any person , firm or cor-
poration liable therefor to pay the taxes within fif-
teen days after the same has become due , he or they
shall be deemed delinquent , and a penalty of eight
per cent on the amount of license tax due shall
attach , and the Auditor shall at once cause such
proceeding to be instituted for the collection of such
license , with such interest and penalties as may be
provided by law for the collection of other taxes .
5. There is hereby created a distilled spirits
Permit Department of the State Government , which
department shall be under the supervision and
control of the State Tax Commission which may
adopt rules and regulations to set up said depart-
ment and to carry out the terms and provisions of
so much of this Act as relates to said department .
Provided that no additional employes shall be en-
gaged by the State Tax Commission and no increase
in salary of the present employees shall be given to
carry out and put into force and effect said Permit
Department . Said department is hereby authorized
and empowered on the date of the effective repeal
of the Eighteenth Amendment to issue permits to
any firm , corporation , association , co -partnership or
individual engaged or proposing to engage in the
business of manufacturing distilled spirits , known
as whiskey or brandy , or other species of such
double stamped spirits in this State ; to manufac-
ture , transport and sell same for medicinal pur-
poses .
§ 6. Every corporation , association , co -partner-
ship or individual engaged or proposing to engage
in the business or occupation of manufacturing dis-
tilled spirits , known as whiskey or brandy , or other
species of such double stamped spirits in this State ,
from after the date of the effective repeal of the
Eighteenth Amendment , shall obtain from the State

12 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Tax Commission a permit to engage in the busi-
ness or occupation of manufacturing , transporting
and selling distilled spirits , known as whiskey or
brandy, or other species of such double stamped
spirits in this State for medicinal purposes , and
upon the issual of said permit shall , in addition to
the taxes now imposed by law , pay to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky a license tax of two cents ( 2c )
on every proof gallon of said distilled spirits for
which said permit is issued . Said payment shall be
made to the Treasurer , through the Auditor , and a
receipt duly signed by the Auditor showing the pay-
ment of the license tax herein provided shall , to-
gether with said permit , have the force and effect
of a license certificate .
§ 7. All revenues derived from the operation of
this Act , after collected and recorded , shall be cov-
ered through the Auditor of Public Accounts into
the State Treasury and credited to the general ex-
penditure fund , and used to match funds provided
by the Government of the United States for the re-
lief of the poor and needy in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky to be spent at the discretion and under
the supervision of the Governor of the Common-
wealth until , together with other relief funds , the
sum of three million dollars is raised . When this
is done one-half of said tax is to be used for the re-
tirement of State warrants , and the other half to
remain in the general fund for such uses as may be
authorized by law.
§ 8. Any corporation , association , co -partner-
ship, company or individual who shall violate any
provisions of this Act shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor , and on conviction be fined not less than
five hundred dollars nor more than one thousand
dollars .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 13
§ 9. No suit shall be maintained in any court to
restrain or delay the collection or payment of the
tax levied by this Act . The aggrieved taxpayer
shall pay the tax as and when required and may at
any time within two years from the date of such
payment sue the Commonwealth through its agent ,
the Auditor of Public Accounts , in an action at law
in any court , state or federal , otherwise having jur-
isdiction of the parties and subject matter , for the
recovery of the tax paid with legal interest thereon
from the date of payment . If it is finally determ-
ined that said tax or any part thereof was wrong-
fully collected , for any reason , it shall be the duty
of the Auditor of Public Accounts to issue his war-
rant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky for the amount of such tax so adjudged to
have been wrongfully collected together with legal
interest thereon . The Treasurer shall pay the same
at once out of the general expenditure fund of the
State in preference to other warrants or claims
against the Commonwealth . A separate suit need
not be filed for each individual payment made by
any taxpayer , but a recovery may be had in one suit
for as many payments as may have been made .
$ 10 . The provisions of this Act are severable ,
and if any of its provisions or any penalty provided
by this Act shall be held unconstitutional , such pro-
vision or the particular penalty held to be invalid ,
may be rejected without affecting the remainder of
the act or such part of the penalties as are not held
to be invalid , and the decisions of the courts shall
not affect or impair the remaining provisions of this
Act or any of the other penalties anywhere in this
Act provided . It is hereby declared as a legislative
intent that this Act would have been adopted had
not such unconstitutional provisions of such invalid
penalties been included therein .

14 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 11. Chapter 166 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1926 is hereby repealed and this Act is
enacted in lieu thereof, and all laws and parts of
laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
§ 12. Whereas , the present State revenue does
not meet the immediate needs for the maintenance
and development of the State Government and its
agencies , particularly the relief of the poor and des-
titute , and the State's credit is threatened with im-
pairment , an emergency is declared to exist and
this Act shall become a law and be effective on its
passage and approval of the Act by the Governor .
Approved this , the 26th
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
day of September , 1933 .
RUBY LAFFOON, Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 5
AN ACT relating to the manufacture , sale and alstribution of
beer and wines and imposing taxes thereon , and providing for
the collection of such taxes and their application , and pro-
viding penalties for violation of said act ; and declaring an
emergency thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The term "beverages " as used in this act
shall include beer , lager beer , ale , porter , wine ,
fruit juices and other brewed or fermented bever-
ages containing not less than one-half of one per
cent ( .5 % ) of alcohol by volume and no greater
amount than may be legally manufactured , distrib-
uted and sold for beverages under the laws of this

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 15
Commonwealth or the laws of the United States of
America , but shall not include such generally recog-
nized intoxicating liquors as whiskey, gin , rum and
similar spirituous liquors .
The term " distributor " as used in this act means
any person who sells , imports , or causes to be im-
ported into the State for distribution or sale within
the State any beverage as defined herein , and , also ,
any person who sells or distributes any beverage
manufacturd , brewed , fermented or compounded by
such person within this State .
The term "person " as used in this act includes
any individual , firm , partnership , association , cor-
poration , or other group or combination acting as
a unit .
The terms " Commission , " " Tax Commission , "
and "State Tax Commission " are each to be con-
strued for purposes of this act as meaning the Ken-
tucky State Tax Commission .
The term " retailer " is hereby defined for the
purposes of this act as a person making sale at
retail .
" Sale at retail " as used in this act means the
sale to a consumer , or to any person for any pur-
pose other than resale .
The term " manufacturer " is defined for purposes
of this act as a person engaged in the production
and/or bottling of beverages .
The term " jobber " is hereby defined for purposes
of this act as one not a manufacturer , regularly
engaged in making sales of beverages intended for
resale .
The term " broker " as used in this act includes
any person acting for or on behalf of any manufac
turer , jobber or wholesaler , whether domestic or
foreign .

16 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The term " sell at wholesale " for the purposes of
this act shall include the selling by any manufac-
turer , distributor , jobber , broker, agent , or other
person , whether or not enumerated in this act who
distributes the beverages enumerated in this act for
the purpose of being sold at retail .
The term " wholesaler " as used in this act in-
cludes any person who sells at wholesale .
§ 2. An annual license tax in the sum of five
hundred dollars ( $500 ) is hereby imposed on every
person engaged in the manufacture of beverages .
On payment of the tax , a license shall be issued for
a period ending on the next succeeding 30th day of
June and annually thereafter .
3. An annual license tax is hereby imposed on
every person who sells beverages at wholesale to
other persons licensed under the provisions of this
act to sell at retail , in the amount of one hundred
dollars ( $ 100 ) . On payment of the tax , a license
shall be issued for a period ending on the next suc-
ceeding 30th day of June and annually thereafter .
If any manufacturer or wholesaler maintains more
than one place of business or warehouse , agent , dis-
tributor , broker or jobber from which orders are
received or beverages are distributed , a separate
license shall be paid for each separate place of
business , warhouse , agent , distributor , broker or
jobber .
4. An annual license tax is hereby imposed on
every person who sells beverages at retail in the
amount of twenty -five dollars ( $ 25 ) for each store
or place of business operated by such person . On
payment of the tax, a license shall be issued for a
period ending on the next succeeding 30th day of
June and annually thereafter .
42. A tax of $25.00 is hereby imposed upon
the sale of beverages on dining cars or other rail-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 17
road cars , and such licenses shall be issued by the
State Tax Commission . The sale of beverages upon
any dining or other railroad car shall be a misde-
meanor , punishable as hereinafter in Section 14
provided , unless a license be conspicuously posted
in said car .
§ 5. The license tax imposed by Sections 2 , 3 and
4 of this act shall be paid to the county court clerks
of the various counties of the Commonwealth and re-
ported by them to the Auditor of Public Accounts ,
as other license taxes are reported . After this act
shall have been effective for thirty ( 30 ) days , it
shall be a misdemeanor , punishable under the terms
of Section 13 , for any person to engage in the busi-
ness of manufacturing and/or selling beverages
until said license tax has been paid and the license
issued thereupon prominently displayed in the place
of business of the manufacturer , wholesaler , or re-
tailer .
$ 6. In addition to the license tax levied in
Sections 2 , 3 and 4, a tax of one dollar and twenty-
five cents ($1.25 ) a barrel of thirty -one ( 31 ) gallons
and a like or proportional rate per gallon or frac-
tional part thereof on beverages sold in any other
container of more or less than thirty - one ( 31 ) gal-
lons , is hereby levied upon the sale or distribution
by gift or sale of beverages . The said tax shall be
paid by the manufacturer or brewer if he is in the
State , or if not by the brewer's agent or wholesaler ,
if he be in the State , and if neither of those just
mentioned be in the State , then by the retailer .
§ 7. All revenue derived from the operation of
this act , as collected and covered through the Au-
ditor of Public Accounts into the State Treasury
shall be credited to the General Expenditure Fund ;
provided , however , that all of said revenue so col-
lected shall be ear -marked for and be used by the

18 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Kentucky Relief Commission to match Federal
money now available for the needy and unemployed
and to be applied and used for that purpose only.
Provided , further that after said purpose has been
accomplished , that said license tax or revenue shall
be ear -marked , and 50 % of same shall be used for
the retirement of interest-bearing state warrants
against said fund , and it shall be the duty of the
State Treasurer to call said interest -bearing war-
rants for payment as and when the sum of $25 ,-
000.00 has accumulated from said tax for said pur-
pose .
$ 8. Except as otherwise indicated herein , the
administration of this act is vested in the State
Tax Commission , which may prescribe forms and
promulgate rules and regulations needed for the
enforcement of the act .
The State Tax Commission , or any representative
appointed by it in writing , is hereby authorized to
examine the books , records , papers , files , equipment ,
and/or inventory of any distributor pertaining to
sales made taxable by this act ; to examine witnesses
under oath for the purpose of verifying the returns
made by distributors or for other purposes involv-
ing the enforcement of the act , and to examine the
books , records , papers , files , equipment , and/or in-
ventory of manufacturers , jobbers , retailers , or
carriers , for the purpose of determining whether
the act is being violated .
The State Tax Commission shall have the power
to institute legal proceedings for the purpose of
ascertaining the amount of tax due and of enforcing
the collection thereof, with penalties and interest
thereon , and for the purpose of enjoining the opera-
tion of the business of the delinquent distributor
until said tax is paid .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 19
The Commission may at or after the commence-
ment of an action for the purpose of collecting the
amount of tax due and penalty and interest thereon ,
as set out herein , have an attachment against the
property of the distributor or garnishment of his
creditors without the execution of bond upon filing
an affidavit showing the nature of the claim, that it
is just, the sum which the Commission believes it
should recover , and an indication that it believes
collection of the demand will be endangered by de-
lay in obtaining judgment or by a return of no prop-
erty found .
The Tax Commission shall keep a suitable record
of the taxes collected , including preservation of the
original returns made by distributors for a period
of not less than three years.
§ 9. Every person subject to the payment of a
tax under Section 5 of this act shall , on or before
the 10th day of each month , render to the State Tax
Commission a sworn statement in writing , taken
from his books , of the quantity of beverages im-
ported, sold , removed or distributed for sale to per-
sons doing business in this State and to persons
without the State during the preceding month , and
shall at the same time pay into the State Treasury
the tax on that part thereof sold within the State .
Every person acting as brewer's agent , import-
ing , distributing or selling by wholesale or retail
beverages manufactured outside of this State shall ,
on or before the 10th day of each month , render to
the State Tax Commission a sworn statement of the
quantity so imported , distributed or sold during the
preceding month and the names and addresses of
the persons , firms , or corporations for whom said
beverage was imported , distributed or sold , and
shall at the same time pay into the State Treasury

20 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the tax upon all beverage so sold in the State upon
which the tax has not already been paid .
The sale or distribution of beverages manufac-
tured in this State and shipped permanently out of
the State to be sold and consumed without the State
shall not be subject to the tax imposed by this act .
§ 10. Every manufacturer , distributor , agent ,
wholesaler , jobber , broker or retailer , or other per-
son subject to the payment of a tax under the pro-
visions of this act , whether or not enumerated
herein , shall maintain a file of invoices of all bever-
ages sold or received for sale and shall keep such
other records as the Commission may require to
facilitate the enforcement of this act .
Said invoices and written records shall be pre-
served and kept on file for a period of not less than
three years .
§ 11. No suit shall be maintained in any court
to restrain or delay the collection or payment of the
tax levied by this act . The agrieved taxpayer shall
pay the tax as and when required and may at any
time within two years from the date of such pay-
ment sue the Commonwealth through its agent , the
Auditor of Public Accounts , in an action at law in
any court, state or federal , otherwise having juris-
diction of the parties and subject matter for the
recovery of the tax paid with legal interest thereon
from the date of payment . If it is finally determ-
ined that said tax or any part thereof was wrong-
fully collected , for any reason , it shall be the duty
of the Auditor of Public Accounts to issue his war-
rant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky for the amount of such tax so adjudged to
have been wrongfully collected , together with legal
interest thereon . The Treasurer shall pay the same
at once out of the general expenditure fund of the
State in preference to other warrants or claims

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 21
against the Commonwealth . A separate suit need
not be filed for each individual payment made by
any taxpayer , but a recovery may be had in one
suit for as many payments as may have been made .
§ 12. It is the intent and purpose of this act to
impose the tax levied in Section 5 on the first sale
in the Commonwealth of Kentucky over which the
State has jurisdiction . The State Tax Commission
shall promulgate such rules and regulations as are
necessary to make this intent effective and to avoid
the imposition of the tax on two or more sales of
the same beverage .
§ 13. Any person required under this act to
make returns and pay a tax who shall fail to pay the
tax due in the time specified in this act shall be sub-
ject to a penalty equal to twenty per centum ( 20 % )
of the amount of tax due and delinquent and in
addition thereto interest at six per centum (6% )
per annum .
Any person who shall violate any of the provi-
sions of this act for which a specific penalty is not
herein provided shall be deemed guilty of a misde-
meanor . Upon conviction he shall be punished by a
fine of not less than fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) , nor more
than five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 ) , for each offense
and said person's license shall be subject to revoca-
tion at the discretion of the court . The revocation
of licenses may be applied in those instances in
which a specific penalty is herein provided , said
revocation to be additional to the specified penalty .
§ 14. The provisions of this act are severable .
If any provision , section or paragraph thereof , or
if the application of any provision , section or para-
graph thereof should be held unconstitutional or in-
valid , such decision shall not affect or impair the
remainder of the act , it being declared the legisla-

22 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tive intent to tax separately each kind of business
and sales contemplated by any of its provisions .
§ 15. Whereas , the present state revenue of the
Commonwealth does not meet the immediate needs
for the maintenance of the state government and
its agencies , and the state's credit is threatened with
impairment , an emergency is declared to exist ; and
this act shall become a law and be effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
§ 16. All laws, or parts of laws , in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed to the extent of such con-
flict.
Approved this , Septem-
ber 26 , 1933 .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 6
AN ACT to provide for a convention to act upon the amend-
ment to the Constitution of the United States providing for
the repeal of the Eighteenth Amendment ; and to appropri-
ate funds for certain expenses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. A convention , the delegates to which shall
be elected in the manner as herein provided , shall

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 23
be held at Frankfort , Kentucky , in the room of the
House of the General Assembly in the State Capitol
Building on Monday , November 27 , 1933 , at 10
o'clock A. M. , to consider and act upon the ratifica-
tion of the following amendment to the Constitution
of the United States , proposed by the Congress of
the United States to the several states :
" Section 1. The eighteenth article of amendment
to the Constitution of the United States hereby re-
pealed .
" Section 2. The transportation or importation
into any State , territory or possession of the United
States for delivery or use therein of intoxicating
liquors in violation of the laws thereof, is hereby
prohibited .
"Section 3. This article shall be inoperative un-
less it shall have been ratified as an amendment to
the Constitution by conventions in the several
States , as provided in the Constitution , within seven
years from the date of the submission hereof to the
States by the Congress ."
2. The number of delegates to be elected to such
convention shall be nineteen in number , to be elected
from the State at Large . The election of said dele-
gates shall be held on the first Tuesday after the
first Monday in November , 1933 (November 7 ,
1933 ) . At such election all persons qualified to vote
for members of the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky shall be entitled to vote .
§ 3. Except as otherwise provided herein , all
provisions of the Statutes of this Commonwealth
relating to nomination papers , the furnishing and
distribution of election supplies , the preparation ,

24 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
printing , distribution , voting , counting , recounting ,
returning and canvassing of ballots for general elec-
tions and all other election laws of the State shall ,
as far as applicable , and not inconsistent herewith ,
apply to the election of such delegates . The votes
cast in such election for said delegate shall be can-
vassed and certified in the manner now required by
law for general elections . The State Board of Elec-
tion Commissioners shall canvass the returns for
delegates to the convention herein provided for and
shall issue certificates of election to each of the dele-
gates elected in the same manner as is required by
law to be done in a general election for State officers .
§ 4. Each group of delegates shall be nominated
by a petition signed by not less than one thousand
( 1000 ) qualified voters of this state who shall in-
dicate his or her residence . Each petition shall
state the name and residence of each of the pro-
posed candidates for delegates to said convention ,
and shall be headed in plain type " For ratification
of repeal of the Eighteenth Amendment " or
"Against ratification of repeal of the Eighteenth
Amendment . " It shall further contain a statement
that each candidate subscribing his or her name
thereto is in favor of or against repeal of the Eight-
eenth Amendment , as the heading may indicate .
All petitions shall be filed with the Secretary of
State not less than forty -five (45 ) days before No-
vember 7, 1933. No nomination shall be effective
except those of the nineteen nominees in favor of
ratification , and the nineteen nominees against rati-
fication whose nominating petitions have been
signed by the largest number of voters , such desig-
nation and determination to be made by the Secre-
tary of State . The Secretary of State shall certify

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 25
the candidates of each group to the respective
county clerks of the various counties of the State as
is required by law . All candidates or delegates to
the convention shall be citizens and residents of this
State and be at least twenty-four years of age .
5. The election shall be by ballot , by the use
of the same official ballots as are used in the Gen-
eral Election to be held November 7 , 1933. It shall
first state the substance of the proposed amend-
ment , and this shall be followed by appropriate in-
structions to the voter . It shall then contain parallel
columns of equal width , headed respectively in
plain type, " For Ratification of Repeal of the
Eighteenth Amendment , " "'Against Ratification of
Repeal of the Eighteenth Amendment ." The words
FOR , AGAINST and REPEAL in the headings of
such columns shall be printed on separate lines in
type at least twice as large as the other words in
said headings .
66
In the column headed " For Ratification of Repeal
of the Eighteenth Amendment , " shall be placed the
names of the several nominees nominated as in
favor of ratification . In the column headed
Against Ratification of Repeal of the Eighteenth
Amendment ," shall be placed the names of the
nominees nominated as against ratification . Under-
neath the name of each candidate there shall be left
a blank space large enough to contain a written.
name . The voter shall indicate his or her choice by
making one or more marks with the stencil in appro-
priate spaces provided on the ballot . The ballot
shall be so arranged that the voter may , by making
a single mark with the stencil , vote for the entire
group of nominees whose names are in either of

26 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
said columns . The ballot shall be in substantially
the following form :
"Proposed Amendment to the Constitution of the
United States .
"Delegates to the Convention to Ratify the Pro-
posed Amendment .
" The Congress has proposed an amendment to
the Constitution of the United States which pro-
vides in substance that the Eighteenth Article of
Amendment to the Constitution of the United States
relating to the manufacture , transportation and sale
of intoxicating liquors for beverage purposes shall
be repealed , and prohibiting shipment of intoxi-
cating liquors into any State or Territory in viola-
tion of the laws of such State or Territory .
" Congress has proposed that said amendment
shall be ratified by conventions in the several
States ."
"Instructions to Voters :
" Do not vote for more than nineteen candidates .
"To vote for all candidates in favor of ratifica-
tion of repeal of the Eighteenth Amendment , or for
all candidates against ratification of repeal of the
Eighteenth Amendment , make a cross with the
stencil in the circle at the head of the list of candi-
dates for whom you wish to vote . If you do this ,
make no other mark . To vote for an individual
candidate , make a cross with the stencil in the square
opposite the name of the candidate for whom you
wish to vote , or you may write in the name and vote
therefor in the same manner .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 27
FOR AGAINST
Ratification of the Ratification of the
Amendment toAmendment to
REPEAL
the Eighteenth
Amendment
O
REPEAL
the Eighteenth
Amendment
O
John Doe Charles Mick
Richard Doe William Mick

28 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
$ 6. The nineteen nominees who shall receive
the highest number of votes cast at said election
shall be the delegates to the convention . If there
shall be a vacancy in the convention caused by the
death or disability of any delegate or any other
cause , the same shall be filled by appointment by a
majority vote of the delegates comprising the group
from which such delegates was elected , and if the
convention contains no other delegate of that group ,
shall be filled by appointment by the Governor .
§ 7. The delegates to said convention shall meet
at the time and place herein provided , and shall
thereupon constitute a convention to pass upon the
question of whether or not the proposed amendment
shall be ratified .
Delegates to such convention shall receive no com-
pensation for their services , but shall be paid their
actual and necessary expenses .
There is hereby appropriated from the State
Treasury a sum sufficient to pay the actual and nec-
essary expenses of the delegates to said convention .
Such sum to be out of the funds of the State not
otherwise appropriated .
The Auditor of Public Accounts is hereby author-
ized and directed to issue a warrant upon the State
Treasury , payable to each of said delegates to said
convention , for their actual and necessary expenses
when such claim of each delegate is certified to the
Auditor by the Secretary of said convention , coun-
tersigned by the President of said convention , and
after same has been audited by said Auditor as is
required by law as to other claims upon the Treas-
ury . There shall be filed with such claims receipts ,
showing the expenditures made , as is now required
by law.
$ 8. The convention shall be the judge of the
election and qualifications of its members ; and shall

AOTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 29
have power to elect its President , Secretary and
other officers , and to adopt its own rules .
§ 9. The convention shall keep a journal of its
proceedings , in which shall be recorded the vote of
each delegate on the question of ratification of the
proposed amendment . Upon final adjournment , the
said journal shall be filed by the Secretary of the
convention in the office of the Secretary of State .
$ 10. If the convention shall agree by a majority
of the total number of delegates to the ratification
of the proposed amendment , a certificate to that
effect shall be executed by the President and Secre-
tary of the convention , and delivered to the Secre-
tary of State of Kentucky , who shall transmit such
certificate under the great seal of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky to the Secretary of State of the
United States in the manner in which amendments
to the Constitution of the United States submitted
to the legislature for ratification are certified .
§ 11. It being necessary to nominate candidates
for delegates to the convention herein provided for
by petition , and it requiring considerable time to
accomplish such end , and it being necessary that
said delegates be elected at the General Election to
be held on November 7 , 1933 , and the time inter-
vening between now and said General Election being
short for such purposes and all things connected
therewith , an emergency is declared to exist and
this Act shall be and become effective from and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
9/7/33
Approved :
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .

30 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 7
WHEREAS , our great president , Honorable Franklin D. Roose-
velt , and the Congress of the United States , believing agricul-
tural prosperity to be an essential part of any plan of eco-
nomic recovery in this country , provided for a programme of
agricultural rehabilitation and delegated to the Secretary of
Agriculture broad powers to undertake to improve the pur-
chasing power of the farm population by eliminating sur-
pluses , controlling production and /or by other methods raise
prices on certain basic agricultural products to a point which
would yield to the producers thereof a fair return for their
labor and investment , and ;
WHEREAS , Kentucky is preeminently an agricultural state and
Burley tobacco is its chief cash crop , and ;
WHEREAS , the present visible supply of Burley tobacco is
greatly in excess of manufacturing requirements , which con-
dition will, unless well organized and intelligently directed
effort is made to forestall it , result in growers of the One
thousand nine hundred and thirty -three crop receiving prices
therefor less than the actual cost of production , and ;
WHEREAS , the purchasing power of the Kentucky tobacco
growers is at the lowest ebb in the history of our country and
owing to the greatly increased prices of the commodities they
purchase they are unable in many instances to buy even the
bare necessities of life and are facing a most serious condition
of bankruptcy and poverty , and ;
WHEREAS , of all the agricultural products included in the
base classification of the Agricultural Adjustment Act , burley
tobacco is probably better adapted to elimination of surplus ,
curtailment of production and price readjustment than any
other , therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Honorable Henry A. Wallace , Secretary
of Agriculture , be and he is hereby respectfully pe-
titioned to at once undertake to work out and put
into effect a practical plan of surplus elimination
and production control and to use , if needed , the
full authority and powers granted him by the Agri-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 31
cultural Adjustment Act and by the President to se-
cure for the producers of the One thousand nine
hundred and thirty -three crop of Burley Tobacco
the minimum parity price designated therein .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
9/7/33
Approved :
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 8
WHEREAS , The Kentucky State Fair has its thirty -first annual
renewal at the Kentucky State Fair Grounds in the city of
Louisville , beginning today and continuing through Saturday ,
September sixteenth , and
WHEREAS , this annual exposition has brought to Kentucky
fame throughout America and has been of inestimable value
to the agricultural interest in the State , and
WHEREAS , this is the first annual renewal of the Fair since
the inauguration of the national recovery administration , and
WHEREAS , the management has named this the recovery re-
newal of the Fair, therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the people of Kentucky be called upon by
this general assembly to attend this year's renewal
of the Fair which is of interest to every citizen and
tax-payer of the State and that the general assem-
bly call upon the citizens of the State to lend their
moral and material support to this year's exposi-
tion , and
Be it further resolved , that officials of the Fair be
commended by this general assembly for the most
excellent results obtained by them for the One thou-

32 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
sand nine hundred and thirty-two Fair , and that
their efforts for the greatest Fair in Kentucky his-
tory for this year be endorsed . The Clerk of the
Senate is hereby directed to send a copy of these
resolutions to the Secretary of the Kentucky State
Fair.
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
9/15/33
Approved :
RUBY LAFFOON , Governor of Kentucky .
CHAPTER 9
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of copies of the Legis-
lative Digest adopted at the Session of one thousand nine hun-
dred and twelve , and every succeeding session since that date ,
as the official publication for the General Assembly and State
and certain other public officials ,
WHEREAS ; It is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Senator and Representative be advised as to the various
and detailed steps of legislation , and
WHEREAS ; This General Assembly has for the past thirteen
sessions approved and adopted as its official publication "The
Legislative Digest ," a publication under the editorship and
management of competent and experienced persons , the pur-
pose of which is to fully advise from day to day the officials
and public of the Commonwealth as to matters affecting them
in an intelligent and prompt manner ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring :
That the publishers of the Legislative Digest are
hereby directed to furnish daily a copy to each
member and the Clerks of the General Assembly,
and to the heads of each State Department during
the time of this Special Session . That the publish-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 33
ers are allowed the sum of Three Dollars for each
subscription for the Session and the Auditor of
Public Accounts is directed to draw his warrant
upon the Treasurer for said amount when such ser-
vice has been concluded . The total number shall not
exceed (two hundred ) two hundred copies , which are
to be paid for under this resolution .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .Neither approved
or disapproved .
CHAPTER 10
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as
the Chief Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is di-
rected to incur by the Senate of Kentucky , there is
hereby appropriated out of the general revenues of
the State , as a contingent fund , the sum of One Hun-
dred Dollars ( one hundred dollars ) , and for the pur-
pose of paying the expenses incurred by the Chief
Clerk of the House of Representatives of the Com-
monwealth , under the direction of said House , there
is hereby appropriated out of the general revenues
of the State , as a contingent fund , the sum of One
Hundred Dollars ( one hundred dollars ) . Said re-
spective funds shall be paid out on warrant of the
Auditor, issued on requisition of the Chief Clerk of
the Senate and the House of Representatives .
Neither approved
or disapproved .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
FRANK LEBUS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .

34 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 11
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION of the Senate and House of Rep-
resentatives of the General Assembly of Kentucky of the pay-
ment of Per Diem to Honorable James L. Dodge , a member
of the General Assembly.
Be it resolved by the House of Representatives,
the Senate concurring herein :
That WHEREAS , Honorable James L. Dodge , a
member of the General Assembly of the State of
Kentucky has been, and is now, seriously
ill
athis
home in Bourbon County
, Kentucky
, and by reason
of said illness
isunable to attend
inperson
, at

ofthe said General Assembly
of

-three
, and
WHEREAS
, through no fault
ofhis own
, the said

isdeprived

as

atthe
